title: Dryopteris paucipinnata
taxonomy: Dryopteris paucipinnata is a species of fern in the family Dryopteridaceae. It belongs to the genus Dryopteris, which is a group of ferns commonly known as wood ferns. The family Dryopteridaceae is a large and diverse family of ferns, with many species found in tropical and temperate regions around the world. Dryopteris paucipinnata is native to the Himalayas and other parts of Asia, where it grows in moist, shaded areas. Its classification is as follows: Kingdom: Plantae, Phylum: Pteridophyta, Class: Polypodiopsida, Order: Polypodiales, Family: Dryopteridaceae, Genus: Dryopteris, Species: D. paucipinnata.
uses: Dryopteris paucipinnata is a species of fern used in traditional medicine, particularly in East Asia. Its medicinal uses include treating various health issues, such as fever, rheumatism, and skin problems. The plant's leaves and roots are often used to make teas, infusions, or decoctions that are believed to have anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. Further research is needed to fully understand the efficacy and safety of using Dryopteris paucipinnata for medicinal purposes.
